<parameter=code>

        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\nLeft frame (node 3):")

    if n['id'] == 3:
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, let me understand the current state:
- Node 1: c, [[28, 16], [28, 19], [31, 19], [31, 16]] - left frame (charcoal)
- Node 2: W, [[28, 20], [28, 23], [32, 23], [32, 20]] - player white part (now a frame-like shape)
- Node 3: b, [[28, 28], [28, 39], [31, 39], [31, 28]] - center bar (blue frame)
- Node 4: b, [[29, 17], [29, 18], [30, 18], [30, 17]] - blue inside left frame
- Node 5: b, [[29, 21], [29, 22], [30, 22], [30, 21]] - blue inside player's white frame!
- Node 6: g, [[29, 29], [29, 38], [30, 38], [30, 29]] - gray inside center bar
- Node 7: N, [[33, 20], [33, 23], [35, 23], [35, 20]] - player green part
- Node 8: c, [[36, 32], [36, 35], [39, 35], [39, 32]] - bottom frame (charcoal)
- Node 9: b, [[37, 33], [37, 34], [38, 34], [38, 33]] - blue inside bottom frame
So the player is at:
- W part: row 28-32, col 20-23 (5 rows x 4 cols)
- N part: row 33-35, col 20-23 (3 rows x 4 cols)
The left frame is at row 28-31, col 16-19. The player is at col 20-23, which is to the RIGHT of the left frame.
To activate the left frame, I need to be directly BELOW it at col 16-19, row 32-35.
Currently the player is at col 20-23, row 28-35. I need to move LEFT to get to col 16-19.
Let me move LEFT once.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at col 20-23, row 28-35
# Left frame at col 16-19, row 28-31
# Need to move LEFT to col 16-19, then the player will be at row 28-35, col 16-19
# The left frame is at row 28-31, so the player needs to be at row 32-35 (below the frame)
# Currently player's N part is at row 33-35, which is below the frame's row 28-31
# But the player is at col 20-23, not col 16-19
# Let me move LEFT to get to col 16-19
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
